meta.purpose: Trading-day market-open financial intelligence report for Jun; not an automatic broker.
precedentBasis: FRED: source trust, release metadata, composable indicators
Bloomberg/Koyfin/TradingView: market cockpit, watchlists, cross-asset tape
BIS/Treasury: liquidity, credit, fiscal plumbing, data dictionaries
World Bank/JPMorgan/BlackRock: report-native chartbook and committee framing
liveDataSources: Yahoo Finance chart API public/unofficial endpoint

holdingDataContract.policy: Missing values are explicit nulls with confidence=missing; no synthetic fundamentals or earnings dates are invented.

marketRegime.inflation: Live proxy: breakeven/rates check required; see FRED T10YIE
marketRegime.confidence: Medium for prices/rates; low for fundamentals/news until adapters are added.
evidence: FRED CSV endpoint for Treasury/rates/credit indicators

kostolanyCycle.interpretation: The market is between regimes; confirmation matters more than prediction.
evidence: Higher yields or weak duration pressure valuation multiples and long-duration growth.

holdings.watch: AI capex efficiency, antitrust, valuation compression.
holdings.actionRationale: Keep as core unless earnings revisions or valuation expectations materially deteriorate.
holdings.liveDataSource: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available)

analysisChart.operationalRead: Outside entry zone: wait for prepared trigger or stronger evidence.

analysisChart.caveat: Public-data decision-support levels; not trade instructions. Confirm with live tape, underlying, volume, and risk budget.

valuationExpectation.method: Compare current valuation, earnings revisions, guidance, growth quality, and implied expectations against SPY opportunity cost.
valuationExpectation.status: Partially live: price, market value, weights, recent performance, rates, credit, and SEC filing metadata are active. Forward fundamentals/valuation estimates still need provider or deeper SEC companyfacts adapter.

rebalance.rationale: Levered/speculative exposures require review before adding risk. Core quality bucket remains intact.
addWatch: To be generated by Opportunity Scout from live regime + valuation screens.
doNotDo: Do not turn this into automatic trading.
Do not use mock data for decisions.
Do not assign BUY/SELL language; use allowed action signals only.

opportunityScout.method: Generated from active market force fields and public quote/rates context; candidates remain research-only until evidence gates pass.

candidates.name: Broadcom
candidates.thesis: Picks-and-shovels exposure to AI networking and custom ASIC demand with a more diversified enterprise/software profile than pure GPU exposure.
candidates.whyNow: If AI capex stays strong but mega-cap platform exposure is already high, Broadcom gives a different layer of the same force field.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:59.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: AI semiconductor growth offsets cyclicality
Debt/software integration risk remains controlled
Price action is not purely multiple expansion

candidates.name: Dynatrace
candidates.thesis: Profitable SaaS platform at 25x FCF with 18.8% revenue growth and $530M annual free cash flow — the closest analog to ServiceNow's profile from a week ago. As AI makes software architectures more complex and distributed, cloud observability becomes mandatory enterprise infrastructure rather than optional tooling. Dynatrace serves the large-enterprise end that Datadog hasn't fully penetrated, and its valuation has reset significantly relative to peers (DDOG trades at 86x FCF, CRWD at 144x).
candidates.whyNow: The sector re-rated +20-49% last week but DT only moved +15.6%, leaving it still cheaper than NOW was at its entry point. Real FCF, real margins, and a structural AI tailwind that isn't consensus yet.
dataSupport: Sector rally: DT +15.6%, NOW +25%, SNOW +49%, DDOG +21%
confirmBeforeAdd: Enterprise win rate vs Datadog in large accounts is holding or improving
AI platform expansion (Davis AI) is driving NRR expansion, not just base renewal
FCF conversion remains above 25% of revenue
keyRisks: Datadog competition in mid-market and cloud-native accounts
Sales cycle elongation in enterprise IT budget freezes
Multiple re-compression if growth decelerates below 15%

candidates.name: The Trade Desk
candidates.thesis: The cheapest name in the enterprise-SaaS/platform cohort at 12.7x FCF for 18.5% revenue growth — trading at a steep discount because of a significant earnings miss in late 2024 that damaged management credibility. If that was an execution stumble rather than a structural problem, the asymmetry is real: CTV advertising is still growing, programmatic is becoming AI-driven, and TTD is the dominant independent DSP with no platform conflict of interest (unlike Google/Amazon). Evidence-gated: requires confirmation that the miss was one-time before promotion.
candidates.whyNow: Fell -5.3% while the entire peer group rallied 20-49% last week — the cheapest it has been relative to the cohort since the miss. 12.7x FCF is a very different conversation if execution recovers.
dataSupport: P/FCF 12.7x — cheapest in SaaS/platform cohort by wide margin
confirmBeforeAdd: Q1/Q2 2026 results show execution recovery from late-2024 miss
CTV advertising growth and UID2 adoption trajectory is intact
Management guidance credibility has been re-established
keyRisks: Late-2024 earnings miss may signal structural rather than one-time problem

candidates.name: GE Vernova
candidates.thesis: A creative infrastructure candidate for the electricity scarcity layer: grid hardware, gas turbines, and electrification may be a more durable AI-adjacent bottleneck than another software/platform add.
candidates.whyNow: Bad-market days can expose which physical-infrastructure names are merely crowded versus structurally supported; watch for relative strength or controlled pullback.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:54.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Orders/backlog support multi-year grid demand
Margin execution is improving rather than just narrative-driven
Entry price is not stretched versus industrial peers

candidates.name: Vertiv
candidates.thesis: Infrastructure-side exposure to AI/data-center buildout: power, cooling, and uptime constraints rather than model/platform winners.
candidates.whyNow: The portfolio already has cloud and power exposure; VRT tests whether physical bottlenecks deserve a dedicated research lane.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:53.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Backlog/order growth supports the AI infrastructure thesis
Margins remain resilient as capacity scales
Entry price compensates for crowded theme risk

candidates.name: Eaton
candidates.thesis: A less flashy but potentially higher-quality way to express the grid/data-center power constraint without owning only the hottest AI names.
candidates.whyNow: If mega-cap AI sells off but electrical infrastructure holds relative support, ETN can be a cleaner compounder candidate.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:33.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Data-center/electrical segment growth remains durable
Valuation reset offers margin of safety
Balance sheet and cash conversion remain strong

candidates.name: Quanta Services
candidates.thesis: A picks-and-shovels candidate for the physical grid buildout needed by electrification, data centers, and energy transition.
candidates.whyNow: If market weakness is concentrated in high-duration tech, infrastructure services may become a relative-quality watchlist lane.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:57:49.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Backlog and utility capex visibility remain strong
Labor/input cost risk is controlled
Price enters prepared add zone

candidates.name: Cameco
candidates.thesis: A creative non-AI software expression of the power scarcity thesis: nuclear fuel exposure if electricity demand and energy security remain structurally important.
candidates.whyNow: If the market punishes crowded AI infrastructure, uranium/nuclear may offer a different supply-constrained power lane.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:58.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Uranium contracting/pricing remains supportive
Position size respects commodity cyclicality
Entry is not late-cycle momentum chasing

candidates.name: Nextracker
candidates.thesis: More experimental energy-infrastructure idea: not just AI power demand, but grid-edge hardware and utility-scale solar deployment efficiency.
candidates.whyNow: Bad market tape can create indiscriminate selling in smaller infrastructure names; only interesting if business quality survives the selloff.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:55:36.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Bookings/backlog support growth
Margins are not peaking cyclically
Policy/tariff risks are understood

candidates.name: TransMedics
candidates.thesis: A genuinely different growth infrastructure idea: organ transplant logistics and preservation network effects, far from current AI/platform concentration.
candidates.whyNow: Adds a non-correlated healthcare infrastructure lane for research when tech/risk sentiment is weak.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:57:25.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Procedure growth and reimbursement remain supportive
Margins scale with network utilization
Competition and execution risks are understood

candidates.name: Palantir
candidates.thesis: A controversial but interesting candidate: if AI shifts from model infrastructure to operational deployment, PLTR may be a pure workflow/ontology beneficiary.
candidates.whyNow: Watch whether it holds relative strength during market weakness; if not, valuation risk dominates the thesis.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:59.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Revenue growth and margin quality justify valuation
Commercial adoption is not hype-only
Entry follows valuation reset or major evidence upgrade

candidates.name: Rocket Lab
candidates.theme: Space infrastructure / launch and satellite systems
candidates.thesis: A creative industrial-space candidate: asymmetric if launch cadence, defense/customer demand, and satellite systems compound into infrastructure status.
candidates.whyNow: Risk-off days can separate fragile story stocks from names still building backlog and execution credibility.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:56.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Launch cadence/backlog improves
Gross margin path is credible
Risk budget treats it as speculative growth

candidates.name: Reddit
candidates.theme: Human data / AI licensing / social attention graph
candidates.thesis: Experimental attention/data asset: interesting if human conversation data becomes a scarce AI-training/licensing input and ad monetization keeps improving.
candidates.whyNow: Creative scout lane: not a core buy, but a differentiated data-rights/attention graph candidate to monitor on sharp pullbacks.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:37.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Revenue growth and margin path are improving
Data licensing is durable not one-off
User/community risk is priced in

candidates.name: Hims & Hers
candidates.thesis: Experimental consumer-health platform candidate: potentially interesting if direct-to-consumer healthcare keeps taking share and category expansion is disciplined.
candidates.whyNow: A bad market can compress high-growth consumer names; only interesting if unit economics and regulatory/product risks remain controlled.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:52.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Retention and margin quality support growth
Regulatory/product concentration risk is controlled
Entry is based on valuation reset not social momentum

candidates.name: Oklo
candidates.thesis: Highly experimental asymmetric optionality on advanced nuclear power. This belongs in the creative lab, not the core portfolio, unless evidence improves dramatically.
candidates.whyNow: Useful as a small-watch object for the future power scarcity narrative, especially on broad risk-off days when speculative optionality reprices.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:57.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Licensing/regulatory milestones are concrete
Cash runway and dilution risk are understood
Position is capped as venture-style optionality

candidates.name: Beta Technologies
candidates.thesis: Asymmetric optionality on aviation electrification: $36M revenue growing 136% YoY, $0.7B market cap. Beta is building the charging infrastructure and power network for electric aircraft before the planes exist at scale — similar positioning to early EV charging networks. The differentiated angle is infrastructure-first rather than aircraft-first. If electric aviation timelines compress, the network effect on charging infrastructure could be substantial. Speculative tier alongside OKLO — not a core position, but a legitimate asymmetric bet with a differentiated thesis.
candidates.whyNow: At $0.7B market cap with 136% revenue growth, this is the kind of tiny-cap optionality that doesn't stay this small for long if the category proves out. Cash burn ($313M) is the primary risk to monitor.
dataSupport: Revenue $36M; YoY growth 136%; cash burn $313M; market cap $0.7B
confirmBeforeAdd: Cash runway extends beyond 24 months or funding round de-risks burn
Aircraft certification progress at partner OEMs (United Airlines, Air New Zealand deals)
Charging network installations are growing, not just contracts signed
keyRisks: Pre-commercial: cash burn $313M with only $36M revenue
Electric aviation regulatory timelines may push 5-10 years
Extreme dilution risk if fundraising at distressed terms

opportunityScout.promotionRule: Promote only with source evidence, add zone, invalidation, portfolio role, and risk budget.

keyRisks: Levered products can decay even if the broad thesis is directionally right.
AI infrastructure narratives can become crowded before fundamentals catch up.
Macro rate pressure can compress valuation multiples across long-duration equities.
Unknown speculative tickers need thesis verification before any conclusion.
humanReviewRequired: Any EXIT REVIEW or TRIM CANDIDATE wording
Any valuation conclusion based on unverified data

finalOutput.mostImportantHoldingUpdate: CONL is the highest-risk position; review risk budget.
finalOutput.finalJudgment: Live public market/rates/SEC filing metadata are active. Next layer: SEC companyfacts fundamentals, valuation estimates, news/community ingestion, and alert/state transition automation.

marketForces.name: Rates pressure
marketForces.interpretation: Higher long-end rates pressure long-duration growth multiples and make valuation discipline more important.

marketForces.name: Risk appetite
marketForces.interpretation: Index and volatility behavior sets the risk budget for speculative and levered exposure.

marketForces.name: AI infrastructure
marketForces.interpretation: AI capex, cloud demand, power scarcity, and data-center buildout remain a portfolio force but need crowding checks.

marketForces.name: Crypto liquidity beta
marketForces.interpretation: Crypto beta can amplify upside but is highly liquidity-sensitive and path-dependent through levered products.

marketForces.name: Credit conditions
marketForces.interpretation: Credit spreads are a plumbing check: if they widen while equities rise, risk appetite may be less durable.

marketForces.name: Inflation expectation
marketForces.interpretation: Inflation expectations influence rates, consumer pressure, and valuation multiples.

opportunityScout.thesis: A creative infrastructure candidate for the electricity scarcity layer: grid hardware, gas turbines, and electrification may be a more durable AI-adjacent bottleneck than another software/platform add.
opportunityScout.whyNow: Bad-market days can expose which physical-infrastructure names are merely crowded versus structurally supported; watch for relative strength or controlled pullback.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:54.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Orders/backlog support multi-year grid demand
Margin execution is improving rather than just narrative-driven
Entry price is not stretched versus industrial peers

opportunityScout.name: Vertiv
opportunityScout.thesis: Infrastructure-side exposure to AI/data-center buildout: power, cooling, and uptime constraints rather than model/platform winners.
opportunityScout.whyNow: The portfolio already has cloud and power exposure; VRT tests whether physical bottlenecks deserve a dedicated research lane.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:53.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Backlog/order growth supports the AI infrastructure thesis
Margins remain resilient as capacity scales
Entry price compensates for crowded theme risk

opportunityScout.name: Eaton
opportunityScout.thesis: A less flashy but potentially higher-quality way to express the grid/data-center power constraint without owning only the hottest AI names.
opportunityScout.whyNow: If mega-cap AI sells off but electrical infrastructure holds relative support, ETN can be a cleaner compounder candidate.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:33.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Data-center/electrical segment growth remains durable
Valuation reset offers margin of safety
Balance sheet and cash conversion remain strong

opportunityScout.name: Quanta Services
opportunityScout.thesis: A picks-and-shovels candidate for the physical grid buildout needed by electrification, data centers, and energy transition.
opportunityScout.whyNow: If market weakness is concentrated in high-duration tech, infrastructure services may become a relative-quality watchlist lane.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:57:49.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Backlog and utility capex visibility remain strong
Labor/input cost risk is controlled
Price enters prepared add zone

opportunityScout.name: Cameco
opportunityScout.thesis: A creative non-AI software expression of the power scarcity thesis: nuclear fuel exposure if electricity demand and energy security remain structurally important.
opportunityScout.whyNow: If the market punishes crowded AI infrastructure, uranium/nuclear may offer a different supply-constrained power lane.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:58.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Uranium contracting/pricing remains supportive
Position size respects commodity cyclicality
Entry is not late-cycle momentum chasing

opportunityScout.name: Nextracker
opportunityScout.thesis: More experimental energy-infrastructure idea: not just AI power demand, but grid-edge hardware and utility-scale solar deployment efficiency.
opportunityScout.whyNow: Bad market tape can create indiscriminate selling in smaller infrastructure names; only interesting if business quality survives the selloff.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:55:36.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Bookings/backlog support growth
Margins are not peaking cyclically
Policy/tariff risks are understood

opportunityScout.name: TransMedics
opportunityScout.thesis: A genuinely different growth infrastructure idea: organ transplant logistics and preservation network effects, far from current AI/platform concentration.
opportunityScout.whyNow: Adds a non-correlated healthcare infrastructure lane for research when tech/risk sentiment is weak.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:57:25.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Procedure growth and reimbursement remain supportive
Margins scale with network utilization
Competition and execution risks are understood

opportunityScout.name: Palantir
opportunityScout.thesis: A controversial but interesting candidate: if AI shifts from model infrastructure to operational deployment, PLTR may be a pure workflow/ontology beneficiary.
opportunityScout.whyNow: Watch whether it holds relative strength during market weakness; if not, valuation risk dominates the thesis.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:59.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Revenue growth and margin quality justify valuation
Commercial adoption is not hype-only
Entry follows valuation reset or major evidence upgrade

opportunityScout.name: Rocket Lab
opportunityScout.theme: Space infrastructure / launch and satellite systems
opportunityScout.thesis: A creative industrial-space candidate: asymmetric if launch cadence, defense/customer demand, and satellite systems compound into infrastructure status.
opportunityScout.whyNow: Risk-off days can separate fragile story stocks from names still building backlog and execution credibility.

opportunityScout.name: Reddit
opportunityScout.theme: Human data / AI licensing / social attention graph
opportunityScout.thesis: Experimental attention/data asset: interesting if human conversation data becomes a scarce AI-training/licensing input and ad monetization keeps improving.
opportunityScout.whyNow: Creative scout lane: not a core buy, but a differentiated data-rights/attention graph candidate to monitor on sharp pullbacks.

opportunityScout.name: Hims & Hers
opportunityScout.thesis: Experimental consumer-health platform candidate: potentially interesting if direct-to-consumer healthcare keeps taking share and category expansion is disciplined.
opportunityScout.whyNow: A bad market can compress high-growth consumer names; only interesting if unit economics and regulatory/product risks remain controlled.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:52.000Z

opportunityScout.name: Oklo
opportunityScout.thesis: Highly experimental asymmetric optionality on advanced nuclear power. This belongs in the creative lab, not the core portfolio, unless evidence improves dramatically.
opportunityScout.whyNow: Useful as a small-watch object for the future power scarcity narrative, especially on broad risk-off days when speculative optionality reprices.
dataSupport: Source: Yahoo Finance chart API (public/unofficial; 1m intraday when available); as of 2026-06-02T23:59:57.000Z
confirmBeforeAdd: Licensing/regulatory milestones are concrete
Cash runway and dilution risk are understood
Position is capped as venture-style optionality

opportunityScout.name: Beta Technologies
opportunityScout.thesis: Asymmetric optionality on aviation electrification: $36M revenue growing 136% YoY, $0.7B market cap. Beta is building the charging infrastructure and power network for electric aircraft before the planes exist at scale — similar positioning to early EV charging networks. The differentiated angle is infrastructure-first rather than aircraft-first. If electric aviation timelines compress, the network effect on charging infrastructure could be substantial. Speculative tier alongside OKLO — not a core position, but a legitimate asymmetric bet with a differentiated thesis.
opportunityScout.whyNow: At $0.7B market cap with 136% revenue growth, this is the kind of tiny-cap optionality that doesn't stay this small for long if the category proves out. Cash burn ($313M) is the primary risk to monitor.
dataSupport: Revenue $36M; YoY growth 136%; cash burn $313M; market cap $0.7B
confirmBeforeAdd: Cash runway extends beyond 24 months or funding round de-risks burn
Aircraft certification progress at partner OEMs (United Airlines, Air New Zealand deals)
Charging network installations are growing, not just contracts signed
keyRisks: Pre-commercial: cash burn $313M with only $36M revenue
Electric aviation regulatory timelines may push 5-10 years
Extreme dilution risk if fundraising at distressed terms

watchTriggers: If VIX moves above 22, reduce speculative/levered risk review threshold.
If 10Y Treasury continues rising, pressure-test high-duration AI/cloud multiples.
If HY spreads widen materially, treat risk appetite as fragile even if indices stay green.
If AI infrastructure force remains hot while valuation expands, require stronger evidence before adding.
If an opportunity candidate lacks a clear downside case, keep it as research only.
strategy.strategyPosture: Preserve core compounders, isolate levered/path-dependent risk, scout adds only where valuation, catalyst, and downside clarity align.

fractalMarketAssessment.portfolioImplication: Preserve core compounders, avoid adding single-layer hype, keep levered/speculative products on explicit risk budget, and prioritize thesis verification for unknown names.
fractalMarketAssessment.nextPostureTrigger: Upgrade risk posture only if VIX < 17, credit remains benign, SPY/QQQ breadth confirms, and candidates enter defined value zones with thesis confirmation.
fractalMarketAssessment.sourceCaveat: Price/rates/credit/SEC filing metadata are public-source live. News/community/fundamentals estimates remain limited until adapters or providers are added.

portfolioStory.objective: Make profit by minimizing avoidable risk: protect core, keep tactical risk small, verify unknowns, and only add where value zone + thesis + portfolio fit align.
portfolioStory.currentStory: Protected core carries most weight; Speculative thesis-verification bucket is meaningful and must be proven.

buckets.label: Protected core
buckets.posture: compound / hold with defined risk triggers

buckets.label: Tactical risk
buckets.posture: small bucket; trim/exit discipline matters more than thesis loyalty

buckets.label: Unknown thesis
buckets.posture: freeze adds until filings/liquidity/downside are verified

buckets.label: Other exposure
buckets.posture: monitor for overlap and opportunity cost

riskQueue.rule: Use strength to reduce fragility; do not average down below invalidation.
Chart can guide timing, but action is frozen until thesis/liquidity/downside are verified.
Use strength to reduce fragility; do not average down below invalidation.
Chart can guide timing, but action is frozen until thesis/liquidity/downside are verified.

allowedNow: Protect profitable/core positions unless thesis or macro regime breaks.
Investigate unclear/speculative holdings before adding capital.
Use prepared zones; do not chase single-day strength.
Treat levered products as short-horizon risk instruments.
forbiddenNow: No add-candidate upgrade without downside case.
No portfolio-risk increase from one-layer chart excitement.
No paid-data assumption unless source is actually connected.
